system.biography: The Porcelain Dolls are twelve servants bound by Helga's blood magic, each wearing a porcelain mask and carrying a different weapon. They stand perfectly still in the village square around the Porcelain Princess, moving only when she commands. These were once ordinary villagers from Rot who fell under Helga's control through blood magic rituals. Now they serve as the Princess's elite guard, their individual personalities and wills suppressed by the enchantment. Each Doll carries one of six weapon types: spears (attack from 10 feet), double weapons (attack twice), great blades (+1 Wound), whips (disarm opponents), poisoned blades (victim takes 1 Wound next Exchange), or shields (+1 Defense). When combat begins in the village square, mind-controlled villagers complicate the battlefield by getting in the way. If the Infusion of Purity is splashed on the Dolls, they're freed from blood magic and either flee in panic or turn on the Princess, confused and terrified. Behind their masks, they're just ordinary people forced to commit terrible acts. Their voices sometimes break through the enchantment with pleas for help or expressions of horror at what they're being made to do.
descriptors: Our wills are not our own
Behind the masks we are just ordinary people

gifts.name: Armed Guard
gifts.description: Each Porcelain Doll carries one of six weapon types (roll 1d6 to determine): **1-Spear:** Can attack from 10 feet away. **2-Double Weapon:** Can attack twice in one Exchange. **3-Great Blade:** Inflicts 1 additional Wound on successful hit. **4-Whip:** Can disarm opponents (contested Check). **5-Soaked Weapon:** Poisoned blade—victim takes 1 Wound at start of next Exchange. **6-Shield:** Has +1 Defense against physical attacks.

system.notes: There are twelve Porcelain Dolls total, but they typically don't all attack at once. Roll 1d6 for each Doll encountered to determine their weapon type. The Dolls fight with mechanical precision but no creativity—they follow the Princess's commands exactly. If freed from blood magic control (via Infusion of Purity), they panic and flee or provide information about Helga's mansion layout. Some may beg the Hunters to save their families who are also held captive in Rot.
